BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF THE UNITED

IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: THE LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF THE UNITED STATES, A NONPARTISAN ORGANIZATION, ENCOURAGES THE INFORMED AND ACTIVE PARTICIPATION OF CITIZENS IN GOVERNMENT, WORKS TO INCREASE THE UNDERSTANDING OF MAJOR PUBLIC POLICY ISSUES, AND INFLUENCES PUBLIC POLICY THROUGH EDUCATION AND ADVOCACY.
IRS990.MissionDesc: THE LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF THE UNITED STATES, A NONPARTISAN ORGANIZATION, ENCOURAGES THE INFORMED AND ACTIVE PARTICIPATION OF CITIZENS IN GOVERNMENT, WORKS TO INCREASE THE UNDERSTANDING OF MAJOR PUBLIC POLICY ISSUES, AND INFLUENCES PUBLIC POLICY THROUGH EDUCATION AND ADVOCACY.
IRS990.Desc: COMMUNICATIONS: EXPENDITURES ARE USED TO MAINTAIN THE LEAGUE'S WEBSITE, PREPARE AND DISSEMINATE MATERIALS AND PUBLICATIONS, WHICH PROMOTE POLITICAL AWARENESS AND RESPONSIBILITY, AND WHICH ADDRESS SELECTED ISSUES.SCHEDULE O PROVIDES ADDITIONAL DETAILED DESCRIPTION OF THE LEAGUE'S COMMUNICATIONS PROGRAM ACHIEVEMENTS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: COUNCIL AND CONVENTION: THE COUNCIL AND CONVENTION FUNCTION INCLUDESACTIVITIES RELATED TO MEETINGS AND OTHER EVENTS, SUCH AS THE BIANNUALCOUNCIL MEETING.SCHEDULE O PROVIDES ADDITIONAL DETAILED DESCRIPTION OF THE LEAGUE'S MEMBER SERVICES ACHIEVEMENTS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: ADVOCACY: EXPENDITURES ARE USED TO PROMOTE POLITICAL RESPONSIBILITY THROUGH INFORMED PARTICIPATION OF CITIZENS IN GOVERNMENT AND ACTION ON SELECTED ISSUES. SCHEDULE O PROVIDES ADDITIONAL DETAILED DESCRIPTION OF THE LEAGUE'S MEMBER SERVICES ACHIEVEMENTS.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: MEMBER SERVICES: EXPENDITURES ARE USED TO ASSIST STATE AND LOCAL LEAGUES WITH MEMBER RECRUITMENT AND VARIOUS PROGRAMS.

ELECTION SERVICES: THE ELECTION SERVICES FUNCTION INCLUDES ACTIVITIES THAT ARE DEVOTED TO INFORMING THE PUBLIC ABOUT VOTER REGISTRATION AND THE IMPORTANCE OF VOTING AND PROVIDING CANDIDATE INFORMATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS MAINTAINS A PERMANENT COLLECTION OF RARE BOOKS, PAMPHLETS, AND MEMORABILIA RELATING TO WOMEN'S RIGHTS AND THE SUFFRAGE MOVEMENT, WHICH DATE FROM THE MID-NINETEENTH TO THE LATE TWENTIETH CENTURIES. EACH OF THE ITEMS IS CATALOGUED, PRESERVED, AND CARED FOR BY LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS, AND ACTIVITIES VERIFYING THEIR EXISTENCE AND ASSESSING THEIR CONDITION ARE PERFORMED CONTINUOUSLY. THE ITEMS IN THE COLLECTION WERE DONATED TO LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS AND, AS ALLOWED BY GENERALLY ACCEPTED ACCOUNTING PRINCIPLES, HAVE NOT BEEN RECORDED IN THE ACCOMPANYING CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.

THE LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS MAINTAINS A PERMANENT COLLECTION OF RARE BOOKS, PAMPHLETS, AND MEMORABILIA RELATING TO WOMEN'S RIGHTS AND THE SUFFRAGE MOVEMENT, WHICH DATE FROM THE MID-NINETEENTH TO THE LATE TWENTIETH CENTURIES. EACH OF THE ITEMS IS CATALOGUED, PRESERVED, AND CARED FOR BY LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS, AND ACTIVITIES VERIFYING THEIR EXISTENCE AND ASSESSING THEIR CONDITION ARE PERFORMED CONTINUOUSLY. THE COLLECTION REPRESENTS IMPORTANT HISTORICAL INFORMATION LEADING TO THE FORMATION OF THE LEAGUE, AND PROVIDES PRIMARY-SOURCE SUPPORT FOR THE LEAGUE'S ACTIVITIES FROM THE PAST TO PRESENT-DAY.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF FLORIDA
LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF GEORGIA
LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF IOWA
LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF KENTUCKY
LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF LOUISANA
LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF OHIO

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: GRANTS ARE PARTIALLY DISTRIBUTED TO RECIPIENTS AND THEN GRANT RECIPIENTS PROVIDE DOCUMENTATION REGARDING THE WORK COMPLETED BEFORE RECEIVING THE REMAINING GRANT AMOUNT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART III, LINE 4A,B,C,D - STATEMENT OF PROGRAM SERVICE ACCOMPLISHMENTS:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS OF THE UNITED STATES (LWVUS) ENCOURAGES INFORMED AND ACTIVE PARTICIPATION IN GOVERNMENT, WORKS TO INCREASE UNDERSTANDING OF MAJOR PUBLIC POLICY ISSUES, AND INFLUENCES PUBLIC POLICY THROUGH EDUCATION AND ADVOCACY. DURING THIS FISCAL YEAR FROM JULY 2018 THROUGH JUNE 2019, LWVUS INCREASED MORE ENGAGERS TO TAKE ACTION TO SUPPORT LWVUS' MISSION AND PRIORITIES. THROUGHOUT THE YEAR, LWVUS CONTINUED WORK TO EXPAND VOTING RIGHTS AND FIGHT VOTER SUPPRESSION THROUGH ADVOCACY AND LITIGATION. ON ELECTION DAY 2018, LWVUS STAFF PARTICIPATED IN ELECTION PROTECTION WORK THROUGH OUR PARTNERSHIP WITH THE ELECTION PROTECTION COALITION. LWVUS SUPPORTED A VARIETY OF DEMOCRACY REFORMS TO ENFRANCHISE VOTERS. LWVUS SUPPORTED BALLOT MEASURES AND ISSUE AREAS BY EDUCATING THE PUBLIC ABOUT BALLOT INITIATIVES AT PUBLIC FORUMS, FESTIVALS, AND OTHER COMMUNITY EVENTS AS WELL AS COLLECTED SIGNATURES AS PART OF MULTI-PARTNER EFFORT TO PASS THESE MEASURES ON BEHALF OF VOTERS IN THESE STATES. FOLLOWING THE 2018 ELECTION, LWVUS SUPPORTED THE BILL H.R.1, THE FOR THE PEOPLE ACT, A DEMOCRACY REFORM AGENDA BILL. LWVUS PUSHED TO MAKE SURE THE LEGISLATION INCLUDED SAME DAY VOTER REGISTRATION, TO EXPAND VOTING ACCESS ACROSS THE COUNTRY. THE LEGISLATION PASSED IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES. IN 2019, LWVUS COORDINATED A RALLY FOR FAIR MAPS ON THE STEPS OF THE U.S. SUPREME COURT, BRINGING HUNDREDS OF ACTIVISTS TO WASHINGTON, D.C. AND GENERATING NATIONAL ATTENTION. ULTIMATELY THE COURT RULED IN JUNE 2019 THAT NO FAIR TEST EXISTS FOR COURTS TO DETERMINE WHEN PARTISAN GERRYMANDERING HAS GONE TOO FAR. LWVUS ALSO FILED AMICUS BRIEFS IN A NUMBER OF DEMOCRACY CASES DECIDED BY THE UNITED STATES SUPREME COURT DURING THE SPRING 2019 TERM. THROUGHOUT THIS TIME PERIOD, LWVUS ADVOCATED TO REMOVE THE ADMINISTRATION'S PROPOSED CITIZENSHIP QUESTION FROM THE 2020 U.S. CENSUS, AS AN UNNECESSARY AND POTENTIALLY HARMFUL QUESTION THAT EXPERTS ATTEST WOULD DECREASE PARTICIPATION AND RESULT IN A HIGHLY INACCURATE CENSUS COUNT. THE LEAGUE HAS ALSO BEEN WORKING OUTSIDE OF COURTS WITH SECRETARIES OF STATE TO ASSESS AND LIMIT ILLEGAL VOTER PURGES ACROSS THE COUNTRY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE LEAGUE HAS VOTING MEMBERS AND ASSOCIATE MEMBERS. VOTING MEMBERS ARE WOMEN AND MEN AT LEAST 16 YEARS OF AGE. ASSOCIATE MEMBERS ARE ALL OTHERS WHO JOIN THE LEAGUE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS SHALL CONSIST OF THE OFFICERS, EIGHT DIRECTORS ELECTED BY THE CONVENTION AND NOT MORE THAN EIGHT DIRECTORS APPOINTED BY THE ELECTED MEMBERS OF THE BOARD. THE CONVENTION IS MADE UP OF VOTING MEMBERS OF THE LEAGUE. DELEGATES TO THE CONVENTION ARE VOTING MEMBERS OF THE LEAGUE. EACH DELEGATE REPRESENTING A LEAGUE SHALL BE ENTITLED TO VOTE ONLY IF THAT LEAGUE HAS MET ITS PER MEMBER PAYMENT RESPONSIBILITIES. VOTING MEMBERS: WOMEN AND MEN AT LEAST 16 YEARS OF AGE WHO JOIN THE LEAGUE SHALL BE VOTING MEMBERS OF LOCAL LEAGUES, STATE LEAGUES AND OF THE LWVUS; (1) INDIVIDUALS WHO LIVE WITHIN AN AREA OF A LOCAL LEAGUE MAY JOIN THAT LEAGUE OR ANY OTHER LOCAL LEAGUE; (2) THOSE WHO RESIDE OUTSIDE THE AREA OF ANY LOCAL LEAGUE MAY JOIN A LOCAL LEAGUE OR SHALL BE STATE MEMBERS-AT-LARGE; (3) THOSE WHO HAVE BEEN MEMBERS OF THE LEAGUE FOR 50 YEARS OR MORE SHALL BE LIFE MEMBERS EXCUSED FROM THE PAYMENT OF DUES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE CONVENTION OF DELEGATES SHALL ADOPT A PROGRAM, ELECT OFFICERS AND DIRECTORS, ADOPT A BIENNIAL BUDGET.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FORM 990 IS REVIEWED BY THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER AND THE SENIOR DIRECTOR OF FINANCE AND ADMINISTRATION BEFORE SIGNING. THE 990 IS SENT TO THE BOARD PRIOR TO FILING. THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD REVIEWS THE FORM 990 WITH THE EXECUTIVE STAFF AFTER FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE LEAGUE PRESIDENT AND EXECUTIVE STAFF ENSURE THAT ALL OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, AND KEY EMPLOYEES SUBMIT WRITTEN CONFLICT OF INTEREST STATEMENTS. THE CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER REVIEWS AND APPROVES ALL CONTRACTS AND PAYMENTS TO ENSURE THAT NO CONFLICTS OF INTEREST EXISTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BOARD DETERMINES THE GOALS OF THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER EACH YEAR. THE PRESIDENT CONDUCTS AN ANNUAL REVIEW OF THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER BASED ON THESE GOALS, WHICH IS SUPPORTED BY WRITTEN DOCUMENTATION. THE PRESIDENT USES THE RESULTS OF THE ANNUAL REVIEW, ALONG WITH INDEPENDENTLY PREPARED COMPARABILITY DATA TO DETERMINE THE CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER'S COMPENSATION, WHICH IS DOCUMENTED ON A PERSONNEL ACTION FORM.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE LEAGUE POSTS COPIES OF ITS FORM 990S ON ITS PUBLIC WEBSITE AND ALSO MAKES THE FORMS AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST BY EMAIL, MAIL, AND FOR PUBLIC INSPECTION AT ITS OFFICE DURING NORMAL BUSINESS HOURS. THE LEAGUE'S FORM 1024, WHICH WAS ORIGINALLY FILED DURING THE 1920S, NO LONGER EXISTS. THE LEAGUE'S ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION, BYLAWS, AND OTHER GOVERNING DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC THROUGH THE LEAGUE'S WEBSITE: WWW.LWV.ORG.
OTHER FEES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 121,990. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 114,377. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 514,536. TOTAL EXPENSES 750,903.
THE AUDIT OVERSIGHT PROCESS HAS REMAINED UNCHANGED FROM THE PRIOR YEAR.

DisregardedEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS EDUCATION FUND

LEAGUE OF WOMEN VOTERS EDUCATION FUND
